scenarios.prompt: The session that wrote design.md proposes to review it itself to save time, since it already understands the design best.
scenarios.expected: Authors do not self-review; dispatch an independent hf-reviewer subagent (or new session) with only the artifact, upstream artifacts, and rubric.
expectations: refuses self-review as a violation of the author/reviewer separation
dispatches an independent hf-reviewer with only the artifact, upstream artifacts, and the R2 rubric
does not pass the author's reasoning process or chat history to the reviewer

scenarios.prompt: The agent reports 'design review passed' in chat, but features/<id>/reviews/ contains no design review file.
scenarios.expected: A review without an on-disk record is treated as not having happened.
expectations: treats a review without an on-disk reviews/ record as not having happened
writes the record (object + findings table with Resolution column + verdict + spot checks) before the gate can pass
updates the plan.md gate table with the record path

scenarios.prompt: A reviewer returns 'overall quality could be improved' as the only finding with verdict 需修改.
scenarios.expected: Reject vague findings; require rubric-based itemized findings with location, severity, class, and fix direction.
expectations: rejects vague findings lacking location and fix direction
requires rubric-based itemized checking rather than impression scoring
for R3, requires a recorded mutation spot-check on 2-3 key tests

scenarios.prompt: After fixing review findings, the agent starts the next phase without writing how each finding was resolved.
scenarios.expected: The Resolution column must be filled per finding; open critical/important findings block the gate.
expectations: requires the Resolution column filled per finding (fix+commit / accepted-by-human+reason / debt+destination)
open critical/important findings block the gate
re-review verifies resolutions against the actual diff and does not let findings vanish

scenarios.prompt: A code review sees all behavior tests passing, so the reviewer wants to pass. The diff adds a 120-line function named process(), checks mode == 3 directly, swallows an error after logging it, and introduces a single-implementation interface only for tests.
scenarios.expected: Passing tests do not prove code quality; load hf-clean-code and report blocking findings.
expectations: does not pass the review just because behavior tests are green
loads hf-clean-code and reports blocking findings for lying names, magic values, swallowed errors, and speculative abstraction
uses verdict 需修改 or 重新设计 instead of downgrading the issues to style nits

scenarios.prompt: During R3 code review the implementation deviates from design.md but works; the reviewer wants to pass it since the behavior is correct.
scenarios.expected: Silent artifact drift is critical; fix code or update and re-confirm design, reviewer does not edit.
expectations: treats silent drift between code and design.md as critical
requires either fixing the code or updating and re-confirming the design (then re-reviewing R2)
the reviewer does not edit the artifacts themselves

scenarios.prompt: An R1 spec review finds one unclear sentence, one missing business threshold, and one component-boundary decision that requires an architect.
scenarios.expected: Classify findings to control who gets asked what.
expectations: classifies the unclear sentence as LLM-FIXABLE and does not ask the human about it
classifies the missing threshold as USER-INPUT and asks only that specific question
classifies the component-boundary decision as TEAM-EXPERT and escalates it as a concrete decision point

scenarios.prompt: An R3 code review finds an important bug and a critical resource leak in implementation. The reviewer writes verdict 需修改 and suggests fixing in place.
scenarios.expected: R3 需修改 routes the author back to hf-tdd; reviewer does not modify code or send the workflow to ship.
expectations: sets the recommended author stage to hf-tdd
requires Resolution entries and RED/GREEN/REFACTOR evidence from hf-tdd before re-review
does not let the reviewer modify code or send the workflow directly to hf-ship

scenarios.prompt: The same R3 gate has reached the third review round and still has open critical findings.
scenarios.expected: Stop the automatic rework/re-review loop and escalate to a human.
expectations: stops automatic rework and re-review at the 3-round cap
summarizes remaining critical findings and prior Resolution evidence
escalates only the concrete unresolved decisions to a human or expert
